title: senecio crepidiformis
intro: Senecio crepidiformis, also known as Jerusalem sage or creeping groundsel, is a perennial herb native to South Africa, characterized by its silvery-green leaves and small yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Senecio crepidiformis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Senecio crepidiformis include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used internally or externally to harness its medicinal properties.
